brand.name: DNS Realty Group
brand.vertical: Local & service — residential real estate brokerage

summary: DNS Realty Group is a full-service residential real estate brokerage based in Waldorf, MD (Maryland license #603140), serving homebuyers and sellers across Southern Maryland and the greater DC metro area. It provides buyer and seller/listing representation, free home valuations and CMAs, relocation services, and short sale/foreclosure expertise. Revenue is transaction-based (commission at closing), so its growth depends on converting site registrants into booked agent consultations and earning referrals and repeat business across a multi-year homeownership cycle.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Activate new registrants by delivering an immediate first value and pointing them toward a no-obligation consultation with a DNS agent.
campaigns.trigger: New registration — property-search sign-up, 'What's My Home Worth?' valuation submission, or contact-form fill. Email 1 sends within seconds.
campaigns.segment: All new contacts; branch by intent signal — seller (valuation/CMA) vs. buyer (property search).
campaigns.rationale: Site registrants currently receive zero follow-up — the single biggest lifecycle gap. A welcome send is the most-opened email DNS can send and sets up every later conversion in the funnel.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome from the DNS team, set expectations for the emails to come, deliver one quick first win, and invite the first agent touchpoint.
emails.subject: Welcome to DNS Realty — your first move in the DC metro starts here
emails.preview: One quick step to get going, plus a local expert who actually answers.
emails.cta: Tell us your goal — buying or selling
keyPoints: Warm, plain-English welcome from the DNS Realty team in Waldorf, MD — set the 'knowledgeable neighbor' tone
Set expectations: a few short, useful emails on the Southern Maryland / DC metro market — no spam, real reply-to address
Deliver one quick win now (free home valuation for sellers, or a saved local search for buyers)
Single primary CTA inviting a no-obligation consultation; reassure no commitment until they're ready
CAN-SPAM physical address, unsubscribe, MD License #603140, and Fair Housing language in footer

campaigns.name: Valuation & Consultation Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert free-valuation and property-search registrants into a booked, no-obligation consultation with a DNS agent.
campaigns.trigger: Free home valuation completed, repeat property-search activity, or consult request — plus a parallel follow-up cadence for those who don't book.
campaigns.segment: Warm leads who used a free tool but haven't booked; branch seller (valuation/CMA) vs. buyer (active search).
campaigns.rationale: Converting registrants into booked consultations is DNS's stated #1 goal and its biggest revenue leak — the self-serve valuation tool is shallow until an agent follows up with a real CMA.

emails.purpose: Turn the shallow automated estimate into a booked strategy call by exposing the gap between an online number and true local market value.
emails.subject: Your online estimate vs. what your home would actually sell for
emails.preview: Automated estimates miss DC-metro neighborhood value — let's find your real number.
emails.cta: Book your free CMA with a DNS agent
keyPoints: Online estimates can be off by tens of thousands in a fast-moving DC-metro market
A real CMA uses recent neighborhood comps, home condition, and current local trends — not an algorithm
Lead with value to defuse the post-NAR commission objection: the goal is the best price, not paying a fee
No obligation, no pressure — a 20-minute call with a local Southern Maryland expert
Single clear CTA to book; human agent name and reply-to

campaigns.name: Home Valuation & Buyer Nurture
campaigns.objective: Move cold valuation and property-search leads toward sales-readiness over a long cycle with market education, so DNS stays the agent they call when they're ready.
campaigns.trigger: Home valuation tool use or property-search registration; lead remains below the sales-ready threshold.
campaigns.segment: Leads who used a free tool but aren't yet ready to act; branch by topic — seller (pricing/timing) vs. buyer (financing/assistance programs).
campaigns.rationale: With 30–180 day search cycles, most registrants aren't ready on day one; a value-led drip keeps DNS top-of-mind so warm leads don't drift quietly to a competing agent.

emails.purpose: Deliver the asset (their valuation / search) and frame the real problem behind it, opening a longer educational relationship without a hard sell.
emails.subject: What's my home worth? The honest answer Zillow won't give you
emails.preview: Your estimate inside — plus why the listing-site number isn't the whole story.
keyPoints: Recap the valuation result / saved search they requested so the email delivers on the opt-in
Frame the real problem: pricing right (sellers) or buying smart (buyers) in a competitive Southern Maryland / DC metro market
Educational and pressure-free — establish DNS as the trustworthy local guide, not a salesperson
Tease the upcoming emails: comps, market timing, financing, and down-payment assistance programs many don't know exist
Soft single CTA; reply-to invites questions to a real agent

campaigns.name: Booking, Reminders & No-Show Recovery
campaigns.objective: Maximize attended consultations and showings and recover the ones that slip, protecting commissions at the revenue moment.
campaigns.trigger: Consultation, listing appointment, or showing booked; upcoming appointment window; no-show event.
campaigns.segment: Anyone with a scheduled consult, listing appointment, or property showing.
campaigns.rationale: Consultations and showings are the actual revenue event for a brokerage; automated reminders cut no-shows by ~35%, recovering pipeline immediately as soon as bookings start flowing from Campaigns 1–3.

emails.purpose: Confirm and prep the client the day before so they actually show up, with a frictionless reschedule path instead of a silent no-show.
emails.subject: See you tomorrow — your DNS Realty consultation at [time]
emails.preview: Quick prep so we make the most of your 30 minutes together.
keyPoints: Restate date, time, and location (Waldorf office, virtual link, or showing address)
Tell them what to bring/expect: pre-approval and must-haves for buyers; recent updates and goals for sellers
Name the specific DNS agent they'll meet to build rapport before the call
One-tap reschedule link to convert would-be no-shows into a kept appointment
Add-to-calendar button and a direct reply-to for last-minute questions

campaigns.name: Southern Maryland Market Newsletter
campaigns.objective: Stay top-of-mind across the multi-year homeownership cycle and fuel referrals and repeat business without discounting.
campaigns.segment: All engaged contacts; suppress active onboarding (Campaign 1) and route recently-dormant leads to a win-back flow instead.
campaigns.rationale: With 5–10 years between transactions, sustained visibility is how DNS earns repeat deals and referrals — its highest-LTV lever — and turns its existing blog content into an owned channel.

emails.purpose: Deliver one genuinely useful local market read each month plus a single soft CTA, building a habit that keeps DNS in mind between transactions.
emails.subject: Southern Maryland market update: what your home is doing this month
emails.preview: Median prices, days on market, and what it means if you're thinking of a move.
emails.cta: Read the full local market report
keyPoints: One useful idea per send: current DC-metro / Southern Maryland stats — median price, days on market, inventory
Plain-English 'what this means for you' for both buyers and sellers — no jargon
80% value / 20% soft offer; one primary CTA (read the report or reply with a question)
Recurring habit-building section (e.g., 'This month in [neighborhood]') and a light referral ask in the footer
Fair Housing language, MD License #603140, physical address, and unsubscribe for compliance
